key_insight: OpenProver combines LLM-driven planning, parallel worker decomposition, and Lean 4 verification to make automated theorem proving more reproducible and interactive.
engineering_relevance: It provides an open-source, human-guided ATP workflow that can support safer experimentation, ablation studies, and proof search tooling for formal methods teams.
method: Planner maintains a compact Whiteboard and an unbounded Repository for intermediate results.
Planner decomposes proof tasks into parallel Workers for concurrent exploration.
Verifier automatically checks generated Lean 4 proofs, with an interactive terminal for human steering.
evidence_signal: The paper evaluates OpenProver on ProofNet and compares it against a simple baseline, using automatic formal verification to enable reproducible quantitative ablations.
watch_item: Performance may depend on Lean 4 verification overhead and the quality of human or planner guidance during interactive proof search.
paper_title: OpenProver: Agentic and Interactive Theorem Proving with Lean 4
